assertions.description: The CSS alpha() function parses the current alpha(from <color> / <alpha-value>) grammar.
The alpha() function accepts an origin color function and replaces only its alpha channel.
The alpha() function supports currentcolor as the origin color.
The obsolete alpha(color, percentage) shorthand is not the CSS Color 5 grammar.
Setting alpha(from red / 50%) resolves to a transparent red color in computed style.
assertions.test: (() => { const e = document.createElement('div'); e.style.color = 'alpha(from red / 50%)'; document.body.appendChild(e); const c = getComputedStyle(e).color; e.remove(); return /rgba?\(\s*255\s*,\s*0\s*,\s*0\s*,\s*0\.5\s*\)/.test(c); })()
